issues.description: The argument order to `git diff` is reversed from `(from_sha, to_sha)` to `(to_sha, from_sha)`, but there are zero tests for `get_changed_files` anywhere in the test suite (confirmed: `orchestrator/tests/test_git_ops.py` has no coverage of this method). This reversal is a silent behavioral change for the existing caller at `workflow.py:860` which calls `get_changed_files(old_base, current_main)`. With `--diff-filter=ACMR` and reversed args, files that were deleted going from `old_base → current_main` now appear as 'Added' in the `current_main → old_base` direction and are incorrectly included in the returned list. Without tests, this regression is invisible.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a unit test that mocks `_run` and verifies the git command is issued with the correct argument order and filter for the default `include_deleted=False` case. Also add an integration-style test using a real temp git repo: create a commit that deletes a file and confirm `get_changed_files(old, new, include_deleted=False)` does NOT return the deleted file. This also documents the intended direction semantics.

issues.description: The new `include_deleted` parameter — the entire stated motivation for this diff ('rollback validation where we need to know what was removed') — has no tests at all. There is no test verifying that `include_deleted=True` returns deleted files, nor that `include_deleted=False` (the default) excludes them. Combined with the reversed argument order, the `D` filter's semantics also flip: with `git diff to_sha from_sha --diff-filter=ACMRD`, the `D` slot matches files present in `from_sha` but absent in `to_sha` — i.e., files *added* in the forward direction, not *deleted*. No test documents or verifies the intended behavior.
issues.suggested_fix: Add tests using a real temp git repo: (1) commit file A and B, then delete B in a second commit; (2) verify `get_changed_files(sha1, sha2, include_deleted=False)` returns only A-class changes; (3) verify `get_changed_files(sha1, sha2, include_deleted=True)` returns the deleted file B. This will also reveal whether the argument reversal produces the correct semantics for the include_deleted use case.

issues.description: `get_diff_stats` is a new public method with zero tests. Three failure modes are untested: (1) empty output when the two SHAs are identical or the diff is empty — `output.split(',')` returns `['']`, the loop runs once with `part = ''`, neither branch is taken, and the method correctly returns `{'insertions': 0, 'deletions': 0}` — but this is not verified; (2) partial output such as `' 3 files changed, 10 insertions(+)'` with no deletions clause — the method should handle this correctly but it is unverified; (3) malformed output where `part.split()[0]` is not a valid integer — `int()` will raise `ValueError` uncaught.
issues.suggested_fix: Add tests for: (a) typical `git --shortstat` output with both insertions and deletions; (b) output with only insertions or only deletions; (c) empty string output; (d) a mock returning unexpected/malformed text to document the failure mode and decide whether to guard with a try/except or let it propagate.

issues.description: The only caller of `get_changed_files` is `workflow.py:860` inside `_inter_iteration_rebase`, yet `TestWorkingTreeSync` and other test classes in `test_git_ops.py` do not exercise this path. A regression introduced by the argument reversal would be invisible at the integration level as well as the unit level.
issues.suggested_fix: Add a test for `_inter_iteration_rebase` (or a mock-based unit test for the workflow path) that verifies `get_changed_files` is called with `(old_base, current_main)` and that the result appears correctly in the iteration log under `files_changed_on_main`.

sonnet_test_analyst.summary: The diff modifies `get_changed_files` (adds `include_deleted` param and reverses the `git diff` argument order) and introduces a new `get_diff_stats` method. Neither method has any test coverage in `orchestrator/tests/test_git_ops.py` — confirmed by grep. The argument reversal from `git diff from_sha to_sha` to `git diff to_sha from_sha` is a semantic change: with `--diff-filter=ACMR`, files deleted in the forward direction now appear as 'Added' in the reversed direction and are incorrectly included in results, directly affecting the existing production caller at `workflow.py:860`. The core feature (`include_deleted=True`) is entirely untested, and the interaction between the reversed argument order and the `D` filter makes the semantics ambiguous without tests to anchor them. `get_diff_stats` additionally has untested failure modes around malformed output causing uncaught `ValueError`/`IndexError`.

issues.description: The SHA arguments to `git diff` were swapped: the new command is `git diff --name-only <diff_filter> to_sha from_sha` but the function signature and all callers pass `(from_sha, to_sha)`. The only caller in workflow.py (line 860) invokes `get_changed_files(old_base, current_main)`, so the underlying command becomes `git diff ... current_main old_base` — the opposite direction. This reverses which files appear as Added vs Deleted relative to the caller's intent. With `--diff-filter=ACMR` (no deleted), files that were *deleted* going forward (old_base→current_main) now show up as *Added* going backward (current_main→old_base) and will be incorrectly included in the result. The workflow uses this list for rebase conflict detection and iteration logging; wrong file sets silently corrupt both. Note that `get_diff_stats` added in the same diff correctly uses `from_sha, to_sha` order, making the swap in `get_changed_files` clearly unintentional.
issues.suggested_fix: Restore the original argument order in the git command: `['git', 'diff', '--name-only', diff_filter, from_sha, to_sha]`. The diff_filter flag is position-independent relative to the two SHAs, so inserting it before `from_sha` is fine — just keep `from_sha` before `to_sha` to match the function's documented semantics.

issues.description: `get_diff_stats` discards the return code from `_run` (assigned to `_`) and returns `{'insertions': 0, 'deletions': 0}` whether git succeeded with no changes or failed entirely (e.g., one SHA is invalid, the repo is in a bad state). Callers cannot distinguish "clean diff" from "git error", which could lead to silently treating a git failure as zero-change stats.
issues.suggested_fix: Capture and check the return code: `rc, output, err = await _run(...)`. If `rc != 0`, either raise a `RuntimeError(f'git diff --shortstat failed: {err}')` or return `None` to let callers decide how to handle the failure.

sonnet_bug_hunter.summary: The diff introduces one blocking runtime bug and one silent-failure risk. The critical issue is in `get_changed_files`: the two SHA arguments were accidentally transposed in the `git diff` command (`to_sha, from_sha` instead of `from_sha, to_sha`), reversing the diff direction for all existing callers. Because the diff filter semantics (`ACMRD` Added/Copied/Modified/Renamed/Deleted) are directional, the reversal means files deleted going forward appear as Added, so the default `include_deleted=False` filter incorrectly admits them while missing genuinely new files — silently corrupting the list used for rebase conflict detection and iteration logging in `workflow.py`. The new `get_diff_stats` method is otherwise well-structured but swallows git errors by discarding the return code, making it impossible for callers to distinguish zero changes from a git failure.

issues.description: The `git diff` argument order is reversed in the new implementation: `[..., diff_filter, to_sha, from_sha]` instead of `[..., diff_filter, from_sha, to_sha]`. This inverts the diff direction. Combined with `--diff-filter=ACMR` (which excludes Deleted files), the semantics are completely backwards: files *added* going from `from_sha→to_sha` now appear as *deleted* in the reversed comparison and are filtered out, while files *deleted* in that range appear as *added* and are kept. The existing caller in `workflow.py:860` — `get_changed_files(old_base, current_main)` — will silently receive incorrect results: newly-added files on main are omitted and deleted files on main are included, breaking inter-iteration rebase conflict detection.
issues.suggested_fix: Restore the original argument order: `['git', 'diff', '--name-only', diff_filter, from_sha, to_sha]`. The old code had the correct order and this reversal is almost certainly an accidental transposition.

issues.description: The old `get_changed_files` had no `--diff-filter`, returning all changed files including deletions. The new default (`--diff-filter=ACMR`) silently excludes deleted files for the existing call site in `workflow.py:860`, which passes no `include_deleted` argument. In the rebase detection context, if main deleted a file that the branch modified, the deleted file will no longer appear in `changed_files` and the potential conflict will be missed. The new `include_deleted` parameter is a reasonable addition, but the default should preserve the old behaviour (no filter) or the caller at `workflow.py:860` should be updated to pass `include_deleted=True`.
issues.suggested_fix: Either (a) change the default to `include_deleted=True` so behaviour is unchanged for existing callers, or (b) update `workflow.py:860` to explicitly pass `include_deleted=True` and add a comment explaining why deleted files matter for rebase conflict detection.

issues.description: `get_diff_stats` is introduced but has no call sites anywhere in the codebase. It is unused dead code that adds maintenance surface without providing value yet.
issues.suggested_fix: Either add the call site that motivated this method in the same PR, or defer the method until it is actually needed.

issues.description: `get_diff_stats` conceptually duplicates `evals/metrics._git_diff_stats()` (lines 138–165). Both call `git diff` between two commits and parse insertion/deletion counts from the summary line. The new method uses `--shortstat` and returns `{'insertions': int, 'deletions': int}`; the existing one uses `--stat` and returns `(lines_changed, files_changed)`. The evals version is private and worktree-relative (`HEAD`-anchored), which limits reuse, but the parsing logic could be consolidated. As written, two independent parsers will drift independently.
issues.suggested_fix: Extract a shared helper (e.g. `_parse_diff_stat_summary(output: str) -> dict`) that both callers use, or at minimum leave a cross-reference comment so future maintainers know the parallel exists.

sonnet_design_critic.summary: The diff contains one blocking correctness bug and three structural concerns. The most critical issue is an accidental argument-order reversal in `get_changed_files`: the new code passes `to_sha, from_sha` to `git diff` instead of `from_sha, to_sha`, which inverts the diff direction; combined with the newly added `--diff-filter=ACMR`, the existing caller in `workflow.py` will silently receive a wrong file list (added files excluded, deleted files included). Beyond that: the new default filter is a silent behaviour change for existing callers that may cause them to miss rebase conflicts involving deleted files; `get_diff_stats` is dead code with no call sites; and it partially duplicates the `_git_diff_stats` helper already present in `evals/metrics.py`.
